Dear sandeep ,
Greetings from VJC Overseas….!
With reference to the rounds of interview, we at VJC Overseas, feel glad to offer you the position
of digi - at our Hyderabad Office.
Your starting date will be on 23 July 2026 at 09:43 at our Hyderabad office.
The Appointment Letter from VJC Overseas will be given only after producing the documents of
identification, qualifications and experience. The list of the documents has given below:
1.
Proof of Date of Birth and Proof of residence.
2.
Two passport size photographs (Coloured).
3.
Last salary slip(If have any).
4.
Photocopy of all educational documents (10th,12th and Graduation).
5.Resignation Acceptance or Relieving Letter from Previous Employee.
6.Experience letter.
7.PAN card.
VJC Overseas is a service-oriented company with its core business focused on offering Immigration
services such as Visit Visas, PR, and Study Abroad courses for various countries. To ensure the
continued success of the company, every team member is expected to meet the minimum sales
targets assigned by their respective Branch Managers.
VJC Overseas is one of the most reputed companies in India and retains the sole rights to make
decisions regarding employee salaries. We appreciate your dedication and contribution to making
VJC Overseas a trusted brand in the industry.
The employee is giving consent for background check to be initiated by VJC Overseas after the
selection process.
Code of conduct
1.
Probation period
1.1. Your employment will be subjected to a probationary period of 6 months, counted from the
date of your joining. In case of leaving the company during probation, you are required to serve a
notice period of 1 month. However, if your performance is deemed to be satisfactory during the
mentioned span of time, you will be given a written confirmation of you being a member of the
established staff of the company. As an employee, you are required to serve a notice period of 1
month, in case of leaving the company.
During the probation period, if the Company finds your performance to be unsatisfactory, you will
be terminated with/without notice.
1.2. Every Appraisal will be done after completion of 12 months in the company based on the
performance.
1.3 NOTICE PERIOD: 1 Month
1.4 JOB TITLE: digi digital marketing
2.
Dress code:
Monday to Thursday you need to come in formal attire. Casuals are allowed on Friday & Saturday.
3.
Weekly off:
Sunday will be weekly Off.
National Holidays will be declared by the VJC management.
4.
Leave policy:
During the probation period:-
· No leaves granted during the probation period, any leave taken will be considered as loss of
pay (LOP).
· All the leaves should be taken with the prior approval of your line Managers with proper
email. It should be approved with the proper mail. Same day leave request will not be entertained
unless it is emergency and submitted by a documentary proof/evidence.
About the Late login- Details will be explained by HR.
5.
Work ethics:
Timing needs to be strictly followed, your timings are from 10:00 am to 7:00 pm, Monday to
Saturday.
6.
Breaks:
6.1. You are entitled to take a Lunch break of 30 Minutes in a day and two 15 minutes of Tea
Break.
6.2. During the working hours you are not allowed to access any social media applications on your
system or phone - FB, twitter WhatsApp etc.
6.3 Personal phones are to be kept at security/reception and Employee can access during break
times.
7.
Salary cycle:
7.1 You will be paid a salary of ₹10000 (Ten Thousand only) per month.
The monthly salary cycle is 07th of every month, if 07th falls on Sunday or holiday then it will be
processed the next day.
7.2 Your salary should not be known by anyone in the office except the management. If you reveal
your salary to anyone in the office will lead to direct termination or demotion in the salary package.
It should be 100% confidential among the management and you.
8.
Validity of the offer:
8.1. Candidate must produce the necessary documents on or before the date of joining. Final
decision regarding the appointment is subject to the validity of the documents produced by the
candidates. After verification of the documents, the final decision lies with the Managing Director
of The VJC Overseas. VJC Overseas may at any time verify the original documents or call for the proof
of the information provided by the candidate.
8.2. Above offer becomes void on:
1.
Non- compliance with any terms and conditions set forth in this letter
2.
Any negotiations made with the management regarding salary or other privileges
3.
Any fraudulent act or behaviour done by the candidate
4.
Providing false information about the identity or qualification or experience
5.Disclosure of any critical information of the VJC Overseas
All the above mentioned polices are very critical for each and every employee, non-adherence may
lead to strict action or if required, Termination.
9.
ABSENCE FROM WORK
No salary will be paid for periods of unauthorized absence. Subject to you following the absence
rules laid down by the company, normal pay will be continued during periods of authorized absence
due to sickness; subjected to any services criteria that may exist from time to time.
In case of leaving without serving a notice period, you must pay an amount equal to your
1 month salary as a payment of expenses for your training purpose & other expenses incurred by
the company.
10.
RULES AND REGULATIONS
VJC Overseas is a service oriented company based on integrity and mutual trust. In order to maintain
and safeguard the trust and confidence of customers and the public, certain ethical principles and
standards of conduct have to be maintained. Other specific rules and regulations that may apply in
your place of work will be available to you immediately after the commencement of your
employment. These may be altered or modified from time to time through a notice to you or by
Company Circular.
11.
CONFIDENTIALITY
As a condition of employment, all employees are required to sign and return the company’s
Declaration of Confidentiality Agreement that pledges confidentiality on all business matters,
appertaining to VJC Overseas its subsidiaries and its clients.
12.
UNAUTHORISED SOFTWARE AND SYSTEMS ACCESS
You are required to sign a declaration concerning the Company’s rules on the above.
13.
STAFF DEALING PROCEDURES
Personal dealing in securities by company employees is subjected to specific procedures, which may
be amended from time to time, and which must be strictly observed at all times. A copy of the
current guidelines to staff will be made available to you when you commence employment. You are
required to read it and familiarize yourself with it as soon as possible.
14.
DATA PROTECTION
Under the company’s policies, all employees have a responsibility to ensure that computerized data
is accurate and is kept secure. Unauthorized disclosure of personal data is a serious offence and can
result in prosecution. Therefore, you must ensure that you:
• Do not disclose personal data without authority
• Do not transfer client details through WhatsApp or emails.
• Do not save client details in personal phones
• Do not access information or systems, directly or indirectly, irrelevant to your task.
• Lock all printouts, when not in use
• Do not disclose your computer password to any unauthorized person.
Details of every employee are maintained on a computerized database. A copy of your details will
be forwarded to you from time to time and, in accordance to the company policy, a copy will be
provided to you in writing, on request.
As per the company’s policy, it is mandatory for the employee to maintain the confidentiality of the
company’s data and personal records. You must notify the company in writing of any change in your
personal information; such as your address, marital status, birth of children, attainment of
professional qualifications, and so forth.
15.
TERMINATION
a) The Company may terminate your employment with or without cause.
i. Without Cause: In the event that your employment is terminated without cause, you will be
provided with thirty (30) days written notice prior to such termination or in lieu thereof equivalent
to your compensation package for a period of thirty (30) days, calculated on the basis of your last
basic salary.
ii. With Cause: The company, after providing you with written notice, may terminate you from
employment with “Cause”, on the following grounds:
(1) The commission of a crime involving moral turpitude, theft, fraud or deceit;
(2) Conduct that has an adverse effect on the Company’s reputation;
(3) Substantial or continued unwillingness or inability to perform duties assigned to you;
(4) Gross negligence or deliberate misconduct;
(5) Any material breach of terms and conditions specified in this letter;
(6) Absence from work for a period of three or more consecutive days without notifying your
superior officer.
You shall be given an opportunity of hearing within the said notice period. The Company’s decision
in this matter shall be final and binding. If an employee is terminated before 12 months, same
necessary legal action is taken as mentioned above.
b) In the event that you decide to terminate your employment within one year of employment
then the employee either needs to serve mandatory notice period of 1 month of gross salary. The
company will also reimburse training cost involved during the tenure.
In the event if the employee absconds from the service without serving the notice period, then the
company will initiate appropriate actions as per the laws including criminal and civil laws of India.
The company will also give negative report on the background check if the employee absconds from
the service.
In the event that you decide to terminate your employment after one-year period under this offer
letter, you shall provide to your Manager, not less than sixty days’ prior written notice of your
intention to terminate such employment. However, the Company may, at its discretion, terminate
your employment at any time during such sixty days’ period. In the event that the Company decides
to terminate your employment prior to the expiry of such sixty-day period, the Company shall not
be required to pay you any amounts in respect of the remainder of such sixty-day period.
c) During and after your employment with the Company, you will not remove, from the Company’s
premises any documents, records, files, notebooks, correspondence, computer printouts, computer
programs, computer software, price lists, microfilm, or other similar documents, including copies
thereof, whether prepared by you or others, except as your duty shall require, and in such cases,
will promptly return or account for such items to the Company. Upon termination of your
employment with the Company, you shall account for and handover promptly to the Company’s (the
designation of the specific Executive/Officer/Director, etc.) all documents, records, files, notebooks,
correspondence, computer e printouts, computer programs, computer software, price lists,
microfilm, or other similar documents containing confidential information, including copies thereof,
which are property of the Company or which relate in any way to the business, products, practices
or techniques of the Company, and all other property, trade secrets or confidential information of
the Company, which in any of these cases are in your possession or under your control.
The full and final settlement will only be processed to only those employees who has served the
mandatory notice period and left the company on a good note. The Full and Final settlement will be
processed after 45 days from the last working date of the Employee.
Equal Employment Opportunity - Our values embrace the notion of diversity and equal employment
opportunity. As VJC Overseas highly values equality and meritocracy, we are committed to an
employment environment that is free from all illegal aspects.
Discrimination - We always employ, retain, promote, terminate and otherwise treat all employees
and job applicants based on merit, qualifications, skills and aptitude to serve the respective role.
Competence and Talent - We apply this policy without regard to any individual’s gender, race, color,
religion, national origin, ancestry, pregnancy, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital or
family status, veteran status, medical condition, disability or political belief.
Harassment - Harassment has no place at VJC Overseas. We do not tolerate any kind of sexual
harassment or harassment based on gender, race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry,
pregnancy, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital or family status, veteran status, medical
condition, disability or political belief; whether it’s verbal, physical or visual harassment, or a form
of retaliation for any complaint of harassment. All new employees receive VJC Overseas detailed
and are expected to read and abide by it.
Confidentiality, Security and Protection of the Company’s Intellectual Property - Every employee
has a duty to respect the confidentiality of the company’s information and the information of our
suppliers, associated bodies and customers. In addition, you must refrain from using any confidential
information belonging to former employees. We are only to access, use and disclose confidential
information which is necessary for us to have in the course of employees or contractors at VJC
Overseas, unless it is necessary for those employees or contractors to have such confidential
information in the course of their duties.
We also have a duty to protect the Company’s intellectual property and other business assets. We
take the security of our intellectual property, business systems and Company property very
seriously. Good security, working practices and procedures for Company property, in all its forms, is
critical in protecting the intellectual property development that fuels our Company’s growth, the
livelihood of our employees, and our shareholders’ investments. Company files, personal
computers, networks, software, internet access, internet browser programs, email, voice mail and
other business equipment (e.g., desks and cabinets) and resources which are provided for business
use are the exclusive property of the Company. Misuse of such Company property is not tolerated.
Many employees associated to VJC Overseas. are creative by nature and want to continue their
creative activities outside of work. It is mandatory for such employees to receive a written
permission from the Company’s Vice President for his or her organization before developing, outside
of Company, any intellectual property that is or may be related to our current or potential business.
Please speak to your Human Resources representative to assist in following procedures regarding
permissions.
These and other obligations relating to confidentiality, security and the Company’s intellectual
property can be found in your New Hire Agreement.
External Communications - Occasionally, VJC Overseas employees are contacted by outside sources
requesting information about company matters, including Company products and financial
information, information regarding current of former employees, Company projects, and/or other
workplace issues. In order to avoid disclosing inaccurate, incomplete, confidential or proprietary
information, on being contacted by any outside source for any information about the Company, you
should decline to comment and immediately contact General Manager of VJC Overseas and give all
the details.
Substance Abuse:
• Any use of illegal drugs by employees, visitors or contractors at Company premises at any
time of day or night or at any Company’s sponsored event is prohibited.
• Employees shall not attempt to perform work for VJC Overseas while under the influence of
any illegal drug. An employee who must take a legal drug (e.g., prescription medication) due to a
physical or mental disability is required to discuss the situation with HR if there is a chance that the
drug may influence work performance or behavior at work.
Employees shall not attempt to perform work for the Company under influence of alcohol.
Violence - Violence of any kind is unacceptable at the workplace, at a Company sponsored event, or
during any time you are representing on behalf of VJC Overseas. This includes actual physical
conduct as well as threats, intimidation and portrayals of violence in pictures or written materials
(except for legitimate business purposes). Furthermore, weapons are prohibited inside the Company
property and while off site conducting Company business.
Conflicts of Interest - Integrity is one of our ACTION values, and we need to ensure that all of our
business interactions reflect the importance we place on this particular value. The Conflict of
Interest policy sets minimum guidelines within which we all must act to maintain integrity in the
work we do.
As a representative of VJC Overseas, it is important that we use good judgment in how we conduct
ourselves in the marketplace. Towards this end, we are expected to avoid any activity that creates
an actual or potential conflict of interest (i.e., any situation in which our actions or loyalties are
divided between personal interest and Company interests, or between Company’s interest and
those of another. If uncertain of whether a conflict exists, you should consult your supervisor
immediately. The following activities are some examples of conflicts of interest, which are
prohibited for all employees associated with VJC Overseas, including executive officers) without
prior written approval from a member of your management chain or, in the case of executive
officers, approval by the Board of Directors:
• Owning, operating, acting as a Director for or being employed as an employee or consultant
by any business that competes, directly or indirectly, with VJC Overseas or that is related to the
business of VJC Overseas.
• Engaging in any business transaction with VJC Overseas except in connection with our
regular employee programs.
• Entering into a new, or significantly expanding an existing, direct or indirect personal
financial relationship with a competitor, customer, or supplier; however, no conflict will exist in the
case of ownership of less than 1 percent of the publicly traded stock of a corporation. Any significant
expansion of an existing financial relationship will require a separate, prior written approval.
• Awarding a contract or entering into a financial transaction on behalf of the Company (or
encouraging other employees to do so) with a family member or someone with whom you have a
significant personal or financial relationship.
• Engaging in any other employment or extensive personal projects during work hours, or
using the Company’s property in other employment.
• Soliciting company’s employees, vendors, or customers to purchase goods or services of any
kind for purposes not related to our business, or to make contributions to any organizations or in
support of any outside VJC Overseas authorized causes.
• Soliciting or entering into any business or financial transaction with an employee whom you
supervise, either directly or indirectly.
In addition to the above activities that require prior written approval, employees associated with
VJC Overseas (including executive officers) are required to promptly notify their Manager or
Supervisor of any of the following events:
• Soliciting or entering into a romantic relationship, or having a family relationship, with an
employee whom you either supervise, directly or indirectly, have influence over career decisions, or
have access to sensitive personnel data. This guideline helps to ensure fair treatment and judgment
across all employee decisions.
• Acting as a Director for any for-profit organization (however, acting as a director for a for-
profit or non-profit organization that competes, directly or indirectly, with VJC Overseas, or whose
business is related to the business of VJC Overseas, is generally discouraged as a conflict of interest
and requires prior written approval as discussed above).
Kindly acknowledge & accept the offer.
Best Wishes,
Mani Vuttaradi Accepted
Managing Director
VJC Overseas {{}}

shall bring to the table. We wish you a long and rewarding career with us.

Mani Vuttaradi

VJC Overseas
